長尾の森の広大な神域に鎮座し、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に「葛下郡長尾神社、大、月次、新嘗」と記されている式内の古社。このあたりを支配していた[[長尾氏]]の氏神とされるが定かでない。また吉野連との関係があるとされる。『[[日本三代実録]]』に、「貞観元年己卯（875年）正月二十七 日大和国従五位下、長尾神社従五位上」とあり、9世紀にはこの地に鎮座していた。社伝によると、寛平9年（897年）より弘安4年（1281年）までの間に九階進昇。弘安4年には正二位、江戸時代には正一位にまで昇進した。『放光寺古今縁起』（1302年）や『大和志』（1736年）によると祭神は伊勢の[[神宮]]の[[皇大神宮|内宮]]及び[[外宮]]の大神[[天照大神]]、[[豊受大神]]である。伊勢の神宮の真西に鎮座していることから、古くから人々の篤い信仰を得た神社であった。また住吉・熱田・諏訪の神々も祀られている。江戸期の文献では[[水光姫命]]、[[白雲別命]]も祀られたとある。また『放光寺古今縁起』には「長尾神社は葛下郡全体の総社である[[天武天皇]]が[[壬申の乱]]（672年）で勝利した後に感謝の気持ちから葛下郡一郡を当社に献じられた」と記されている。 鎮座地は[[竹内街道]]、[[長尾街道]]、[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]が交差する交通の要衝であり、古来より交通安全、旅行安全の神として篤く信仰されている。[[参道]]は[[拝殿]]に向かって東西に長く伸びており一の鳥居は近年新たに竣工した。 二の鳥居の両脇には「なで蛙」が配されて、参拝者を見守っており、安産祈願の神としても名高い。 また水光姫命は[[古事記]]や[[日本書紀]]に体が光って尾が生じていたと記されており、神様の化身が[[白蛇]]であると言われるところから蛇の頭が[[大神神社]]で尾が'''長尾神社'''という伝承がある。さらに'''長尾神社'''は西日本の[[長尾]][[姓]]の発祥地とも表記がされているが、[[苗字帯刀]]の頃より吉川家が代々受け継いでおり、関係性は定かではない。 ==祭神== 主祭神は[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ）、[[豊受大神]]（とようけのおおみかみ）。祭神は水光姫命（みひかひめのみこと）、白雲別命（しらくもわけのみこと）。 水光姫命は『[[日本書紀]]』で[[神武天皇]][[神武東征|東征]]に際し吉野川上（奈良県[[川上村 (奈良県)|川上村]]井光）に巡幸の際、[[井戸]]の中から現れた[[天津神・国津神|国神]]（くにつかみ）として記される[[井氷鹿]]で、[[水神]]・井戸の神である。[[古事記]]や[[日本書紀]]によると、光って尾が生じていたと記されている。[[新撰姓氏録]]では「吉野氏の祖先で、天白雲別命の娘・豊御富登であり、水光姫の名は[[神武天皇]]が授けられたもの」としている。社伝では、水光姫命は[[応神天皇]]の治世に三角岩（葛城市竹内）に降臨し、子孫の[[加彌比加尼]]（かむひかね）に命じて長尾に祀らせたもので、姿は白蛇であって、今、神社の東北に藤をもって覆われている御陰井の藤の花がそれであるという。 嘉吉3年（1443年）の王寺町放光寺縁起に「長尾神社は放光寺の鎮守神」と記載されており、長尾神社の名称の由来は、「城上郡（今の桜井市のあたり）の美女を嫁にされた神があったが、一向に姿を現さないので、嫁の父が神の着物のすそに長い赤糸を取り付けてその後をつけていった所、長尾宮（ながおのみや）に入った」という話がある。水光姫命は、豊かな吉野川の水を守る水神であり、井戸の神でもある。 白雲別命は水光姫命の父神で、天より降臨したといわれる。神名から水神・[[雲]]の神とされる。 摂社は[[厳嶋神社]]（市杵嶋姫命（いちきしまのひめのみこと）・配祀　天児屋根命）。[[弁才天|弁財天]]を祀ることから知恵の神とされる。 ==伝説== 昔、大和に大きな[[ヘビ|蛇]]が住み、[[三輪山]]を七回り半に取り巻き、その尾は[[長尾]]一帯まで届いていた。ナガモノ（蛇）を祀りその最後尾であることからナガモノの尾、すなわち[[長尾]]（ナガオ）と名が与えられたという説がある。このことから[[大神神社|三輪明神]]（[[桜井市]]）が頭で、長尾神社はその尾にあたると言われており、一緒詣りをすると利益（りやく）があるとされる。 また、[[竜]]が住んでいたとも言われ、[[石園座多久虫玉神社|竜王社]]（[[大和高田市]]）は竜の頭であり、長尾神社は竜の尾にあたるとされている。長尾神社の手水舎を司るのが竜である。また、竜王社が大蛇の胴体であり、[[大神神社]]−[[石園座多久虫玉神社|竜王社]]−長尾神社が大蛇の化身であるという説もある。そのため、三輪明神と龍王宮の拝殿や本殿は西を、長尾神社の本殿は東で向き合っていると言われる。 以上のような伝説から近年では龍神、白大蛇の神々の宿る神聖な巡礼地（[[スピリチュアル]]スポット）としても信仰が厚い。 ==主な祭事== 1月1日[[歳旦祭]]（午前0時ー本殿初祈祷）、2月3日[[節分祭]]、3月4日[[御田祭|おんだ祭]]（[[御田植祭|御田植神事]]と[[御供]]蒔）、7月中旬[[夏祭り|夏祭]]、10月4日[[宵宮]]（初[[絵馬]]奉納）、10月第２土曜日曜[[だんじり]]、11月23日[[新嘗祭]]（初穂祈祷） == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[奈良県]][[葛城市]]長尾471 ; 交通アクセス {{underline|鉄道}} * 最寄駅：[[近畿日本鉄道]][[南大阪線]] [[磐城駅]]下車 改札左手方向にまっすぐ（拝殿まで徒歩約1分） - 準急、普通のみ停車。列車本数は年末年始を除いて1時間2本程度であり、注意が必要。 * 最寄駅：近畿日本鉄道南大阪線 [[尺土駅]]下車 （拝殿まで徒歩約10分） -特急、急行、 準急、普通すべて停車。一の鳥居をくぐり、参道を歩みながら二の鳥居、拝殿へと進むことができる。 ==関連項目== *[[伊勢神宮]]− 神宮から見て真西に長尾神社が鎮座している。 *[[橿原神宮]] *[[神武天皇]] *[[天武天皇]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} *[http://www.meguru-nara.jp/theme/inori/syaji/05/06.html 長尾神社] - 奈良県観光局 *[http://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/index.cfm/14,10470,53,214,html 長尾神社] - 葛城市 *[http://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/index.cfm/14,2591,43,199,html 長尾神社のおんだ祭] - 葛城市 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかおしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:葛下郡]] [[3月4日]]（[[御田祭|おんだ祭]]）
7月中旬（[[夏祭り|夏祭]]）
[[11月23日]]（[[新嘗祭]]） }} '''長尾神社'''（ながおじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]に鎮座する[[葛下郡]]の総社。旧[[大和国]][[葛下郡]]の[[式内大社]]で、旧社格は[[郷社]]。 ==由緒・歴史== 主祭神は[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ）、[[豊受大神]]（とようけのおおみかみ）。祭神は水光姫命（みひかひめのみこと）、白雲別命（しらくもわけのみこと）。 水光姫命は『[[日本書紀]]』で[[神武天皇]][[神武東征|東征]]に際し吉野川上（奈良県[[川上村 長尾の森の広大な神域に鎮座し、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に「葛下郡長尾神社、大、月次、新嘗」と記されている式内の古社。このあたりを支配していた[[長尾氏]]の氏神とされるが定かでない。また吉野連との関係があるとされる。『[[日本三代実録]]』に、「貞観元年己卯（875年）正月二十七 日大和国従五位下、長尾神社従五位上」とあり、9世紀にはこの地に鎮座していた。社伝によると、寛平9年（897年）より弘安4年（1281年）までの間に九階進昇。弘安4年には正二位、江戸時代には正一位にまで昇進した。『放光寺古今縁起』（1302年）や『大和志』（1736年）によると祭神は[[伊勢神宮|伊勢の神宮]]の[[皇大神宮|内宮]]及び[[外宮]]の大神[[天照大神]]、[[豊受大神]]である。伊勢の神宮の真西に鎮座していることから、古くから人々の篤い信仰を得た神社であった。また住吉・熱田・諏訪の神々も祀られている。江戸期の文献では[[水光姫命]]、[[白雲別命]]も祀られたとある。また『放光寺古今縁起』には「長尾神社は葛下郡全体の総社である[[天武天皇]]が[[壬申の乱]]（672年）で勝利した後に感謝の気持ちから葛下郡一郡を当社に献じられた」と記されている。 鎮座地は[[竹内街道]]、[[長尾街道]]、[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]が交差する交通の要衝であり、古来より交通安全、旅行安全の神として篤く信仰されている。[[参道]]は[[拝殿]]に向かって東西に長く伸びており一の鳥居は近年新たに竣工した。 二の鳥居の両脇には「なで蛙」が配されて、参拝者を見守っており、安産祈願の神としても名高い。 また水光姫命は[[古事記]]や[[日本書紀]]に体が光って尾が生じていたと記されており、神様の化身が[[白蛇]]であると言われるところから蛇の頭が[[大神神社]]で尾が'''長尾神社'''という伝承がある。さらに'''長尾神社'''は西日本の[[長尾]][[姓]]の発祥地とも表記がされているが、[[苗字帯刀]]の頃より吉川家が代々受け継いでおり、関係性は定かではない。 ==祭神== 主祭神は[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ）、[[豊受大神]]（とようけのおおみかみ）。祭神は水光姫命（みひかひめのみこと）、白雲別命（しらくもわけのみこと）。 水光姫命は『[[日本書紀]]』で[[神武天皇]][[神武東征|東征]]に際し吉野川上（奈良県[[川上村 (奈良県)|川上村]]井光）に巡幸の際、[[井戸]]の中から現れた[[天津神・国津神|国神]]（くにつかみ）として記される[[井氷鹿]]で、[[水神]]・井戸の神である。[[古事記]]や[[日本書紀]]によると、光って尾が生じていたと記されている。[[新撰姓氏録]]では「吉野氏の祖先で、天白雲別命の娘・豊御富登であり、水光姫の名は[[神武天皇]]が授けられたもの」としている。社伝では、水光姫命は[[応神天皇]]の治世に三角岩（葛城市竹内）に降臨し、子孫の[[加彌比加尼]]（かむひかね）に命じて長尾に祀らせたもので、姿は白蛇であって、今、神社の東北に藤をもって覆われている御陰井の藤の花がそれであるという。 嘉吉3年（1443年）の王寺町放光寺縁起に「長尾神社は放光寺の鎮守神」と記載されており、長尾神社の名称の由来は、「城上郡（今の桜井市のあたり）の美女を嫁にされた神があったが、一向に姿を現さないので、嫁の父が神の着物のすそに長い赤糸を取り付けてその後をつけていった所、長尾宮（ながおのみや）に入った」という話がある。水光姫命は、豊かな吉野川の水を守る水神であり、井戸の神でもある。 白雲別命は水光姫命の父神で、天より降臨したといわれる。神名から水神・[[雲]]の神とされる。 摂社は[[厳嶋神社]]（市杵嶋姫命（いちきしまのひめのみこと）・配祀　天児屋根命）。[[弁才天|弁財天]]を祀ることから知恵の神とされる。 ==伝説== 昔、大和に大きな[[ヘビ|蛇]]が住み、[[三輪山]]を七回り半に取り巻き、その尾は[[長尾]]一帯まで届いていた。ナガモノ（蛇）を祀りその最後尾であることからナガモノの尾、すなわち[[長尾]]（ナガオ）と名が与えられたという説がある。このことから[[大神神社|三輪明神]]（[[桜井市]]）が頭で、長尾神社はその尾にあたると言われており、一緒詣りをすると利益（りやく）があるとされる。 また、[[竜]]が住んでいたとも言われ、[[石園座多久虫玉神社|竜王社]]（[[大和高田市]]）は竜の頭であり、長尾神社は竜の尾にあたるとされている。長尾神社の手水舎を司るのが竜である。また、竜王社が大蛇の胴体であり、[[大神神社]]−[[石園座多久虫玉神社|竜王社]]−長尾神社が大蛇の化身であるという説もある。そのため、三輪明神と龍王宮の拝殿や本殿は西を、長尾神社の本殿は東で向き合っていると言われる。 以上のような伝説から近年では龍神、白大蛇の神々の宿る神聖な巡礼地（[[スピリチュアル]]スポット）としても信仰が厚い。 ==主な祭事== 1月1日[[歳旦祭]]（午前0時ー本殿初祈祷）、2月3日[[節分祭]]、3月4日[[御田祭|おんだ祭]]（[[御田植祭|御田植神事]]と[[御供]]蒔）、7月中旬[[夏祭り|夏祭]]、10月4日[[宵宮]]（初[[絵馬]]奉納）、10月第２土曜日曜[[だんじり]]、11月23日[[新嘗祭]]（初穂祈祷） == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[奈良県]][[葛城市]]長尾471 ; 交通アクセス {{underline|鉄道}} * 最寄駅：[[近畿日本鉄道]][[南大阪線]] [[磐城駅]]下車 改札左手方向にまっすぐ（拝殿まで徒歩約1分） - 準急、普通のみ停車。列車本数は年末年始を除いて1時間2本程度であり、注意が必要。 * 最寄駅：近畿日本鉄道南大阪線 [[尺土駅]]下車 （拝殿まで徒歩約10分） -特急、急行、 準急、普通すべて停車。一の鳥居をくぐり、参道を歩みながら二の鳥居、拝殿へと進むことができる。 ==関連項目== *[[伊勢神宮]]− 神宮から見て真西に長尾神社が鎮座している。 *[[橿原神宮]] *[[神武天皇]] *[[天武天皇]] *[[ヤマタノオロチ]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} *[http://www.meguru-nara.jp/theme/inori/syaji/05/06.html 長尾神社] - 奈良県観光局 *[http://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/index.cfm/14,10470,53,214,html 長尾神社] - 葛城市 *[http://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/index.cfm/14,2591,43,199,html 長尾神社のおんだ祭] - 葛城市 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかおしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:葛下郡]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大なかおしんしや]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagao]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[Jinja (shrine)|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[ja:長尾神社]]

The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]][[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 7]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]][[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagao]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[Jinja (shrine)|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 7]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]][[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagao]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[Jinja (shrine)|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:11]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]][[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] {{Sōja shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagao]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[Jinja (shrine)|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: 59 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:長尾神社 up to rev 102720413
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2013年1月}} {{神社 |名称 = 長尾神社 |画像 = [[Image:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg|220px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[葛城市]]長尾471 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照大神]]
[[豊受大神]]
水光姫命(豊御富)
白雲別命 |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 式内大社長尾神社 |札所等 = 奈良県葛城市長尾神社内 |例祭 = [[10月4日]]（宵宮） |神事 = [[1月1日]]（[[歳旦祭]]）
[[3月4日]]（[[御田祭|おんだ祭]]）
7月中旬（[[夏祭り|夏祭]]）
[[11月23日]]（[[新嘗祭]]） }} '''長尾神社'''（ながおじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]に鎮座する[[葛下郡]]の総社。旧[[大和国]][[葛下郡]]の[[式内大社]]で、旧社格は[[郷社]]。 ==由緒・歴史== 長尾の森の広大な神域に鎮座し、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に「葛下郡長尾神社、大、月次、新嘗」と記されている式内の古社。このあたりを支配していた[[長尾氏]]の氏神とされるが定かでない。また吉野連との関係があるとされる。『[[日本三代実録]]』に、「貞観元年己卯（875年）正月二十七 日大和国従五位下、長尾神社従五位上」とあり、9世紀にはこの地に鎮座していた。社伝によると、寛平9年（897年）より弘安4年（1281年）までの間に九階進昇。弘安4年には正二位、江戸時代には正一位にまで昇進した。『放光寺古今縁起』（1302年）や『大和志』（1736年）によると祭神は[[伊勢神宮|伊勢の神宮]]の[[皇大神宮|内宮]]及び[[外宮]]の大神[[天照大神]]、[[豊受大神]]である。伊勢の神宮の真西に鎮座していることから、古くから人々の篤い信仰を得た神社であった。また住吉・熱田・諏訪の神々も祀られている。江戸期の文献では[[水光姫命]]、[[白雲別命]]も祀られたとある。また『放光寺古今縁起』には「長尾神社は葛下郡全体の総社である[[天武天皇]]が[[壬申の乱]]（672年）で勝利した後に感謝の気持ちから葛下郡一郡を当社に献じられた」と記されている。 鎮座地は[[竹内街道]]、[[長尾街道]]、[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]が交差する交通の要衝であり、古来より交通安全、旅行安全の神として篤く信仰されている。[[参道]]は[[拝殿]]に向かって東西に長く伸びており一の鳥居は近年新たに竣工した。 二の鳥居の両脇には「なで蛙」が配されて、参拝者を見守っており、安産祈願の神としても名高い。 また水光姫命は[[古事記]]や[[日本書紀]]に体が光って尾が生じていたと記されており、神様の化身が[[白蛇]]であると言われるところから蛇の頭が[[大神神社]]で尾が'''長尾神社'''という伝承がある。さらに'''長尾神社'''は西日本の[[長尾]][[姓]]の発祥地とも表記がされているが、[[苗字帯刀]]の頃より吉川家が代々受け継いでおり、関係性は定かではない。 ==祭神== 主祭神は[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ）、[[豊受大神]]（とようけのおおみかみ）。祭神は水光姫命（みひかひめのみこと）、白雲別命（しらくもわけのみこと）。 水光姫命は『[[日本書紀]]』で[[神武天皇]][[神武東征|東征]]に際し吉野川上（奈良県[[川上村 (奈良県)|川上村]]井光）に巡幸の際、[[井戸]]の中から現れた[[天津神・国津神|国神]]（くにつかみ）として記される[[井氷鹿]]で、[[水神]]・井戸の神である。[[古事記]]や[[日本書紀]]によると、光って尾が生じていたと記されている。[[新撰姓氏録]]では「吉野氏の祖先で、天白雲別命の娘・豊御富登であり、水光姫の名は[[神武天皇]]が授けられたもの」としている。社伝では、水光姫命は[[応神天皇]]の治世に三角岩（葛城市竹内）に降臨し、子孫の[[加彌比加尼]]（かむひかね）に命じて長尾に祀らせたもので、姿は白蛇であって、今、神社の東北に藤をもって覆われている御陰井の藤の花がそれであるという。 嘉吉3年（1443年）の王寺町放光寺縁起に「長尾神社は放光寺の鎮守神」と記載されており、長尾神社の名称の由来は、「城上郡（今の桜井市のあたり）の美女を嫁にされた神があったが、一向に姿を現さないので、嫁の父が神の着物のすそに長い赤糸を取り付けてその後をつけていった所、長尾宮（ながおのみや）に入った」という話がある。水光姫命は、豊かな吉野川の水を守る水神であり、井戸の神でもある。 白雲別命は水光姫命の父神で、天より降臨したといわれる。神名から水神・[[雲]]の神とされる。 摂社は[[厳嶋神社]]（市杵嶋姫命（いちきしまのひめのみこと）・配祀　天児屋根命）。[[弁才天|弁財天]]を祀ることから知恵の神とされる。 ==伝説== 昔、大和に大きな[[ヘビ|蛇]]が住み、[[三輪山]]を七回り半に取り巻き、その尾は[[長尾]]一帯まで届いていた。ナガモノ（蛇）を祀りその最後尾であることからナガモノの尾、すなわち[[長尾]]（ナガオ）と名が与えられたという説がある。このことから[[大神神社|三輪明神]]（[[桜井市]]）が頭で、長尾神社はその尾にあたると言われており、一緒詣りをすると利益（りやく）があるとされる。 また、[[竜]]が住んでいたとも言われ、[[石園座多久虫玉神社|竜王社]]（[[大和高田市]]）は竜の頭であり、長尾神社は竜の尾にあたるとされている。長尾神社の手水舎を司るのが竜である。また、竜王社が大蛇の胴体であり、[[大神神社]]−[[石園座多久虫玉神社|竜王社]]−長尾神社が大蛇の化身であるという説もある。そのため、三輪明神と龍王宮の拝殿や本殿は西を、長尾神社の本殿は東で向き合っていると言われる。 以上のような伝説から近年では龍神、白大蛇の神々の宿る神聖な巡礼地（[[スピリチュアル]]スポット）としても信仰が厚い。 ==主な祭事== 1月1日[[歳旦祭]]（午前0時ー本殿初祈祷）、2月3日[[節分祭]]、3月4日[[御田祭|おんだ祭]]（[[御田植祭|御田植神事]]と[[御供]]蒔）、7月中旬[[夏祭り|夏祭]]、10月4日[[宵宮]]（初[[絵馬]]奉納）、10月第２土曜日曜[[だんじり]]、11月23日[[新嘗祭]]（初穂祈祷） == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[奈良県]][[葛城市]]長尾471 ; 交通アクセス {{underline|鉄道}} * 最寄駅：[[近畿日本鉄道]][[南大阪線]] [[磐城駅]]下車 改札左手方向にまっすぐ（拝殿まで徒歩約1分） - 準急、普通のみ停車。列車本数は年末年始を除いて1時間2本程度であり、注意が必要。 * 最寄駅：近畿日本鉄道南大阪線 [[尺土駅]]下車 （拝殿まで徒歩約10分） -特急、急行、 準急、普通すべて停車。一の鳥居をくぐり、参道を歩みながら二の鳥居、拝殿へと進むことができる。 ==関連項目== *[[伊勢神宮]]− 神宮から見て真西に長尾神社が鎮座している。 *[[橿原神宮]] *[[神武天皇]] *[[天武天皇]] *[[ヤマタノオロチ]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} *[http://www.meguru-nara.jp/theme/inori/syaji/05/06.html 長尾神社] - 奈良県観光局 *[http://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/index.cfm/14,10470,53,214,html 長尾神社] - 葛城市 *[http://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/index.cfm/14,2591,43,199,html 長尾神社のおんだ祭] - 葛城市 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかおしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:葛下郡]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大なかおしんしや]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2013年1月}} {{神社 |名称 = 長尾神社 |画像 = [[Image:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg|220px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[葛城市]]長尾471 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[天照大神]]
[[豊受大神]]
水光姫命(豊御富)
白雲別命 |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 式内大社長尾神社 |札所等 = 奈良県葛城市長尾神社内 |例祭 = [[10月4日]]（宵宮） |神事 = [[1月1日]]（[[歳旦祭]]）
[[3月4日]]（[[御田祭|おんだ祭]]）
7月中旬（[[夏祭り|夏祭]]）
[[11月23日]]（[[新嘗祭]]） }} '''長尾神社'''（ながおじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]に鎮座する[[葛下郡]]の総社。旧[[大和国]][[葛下郡]]の[[式内大社]]で、旧社格は[[郷社]]。 ==由緒・歴史== 長尾の森の広大な神域に鎮座し、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に「葛下郡長尾神社、大、月次、新嘗」と記されている式内の古社。このあたりを支配していた[[長尾氏]]の氏神とされるが定かでない。また吉野連との関係があるとされる。『[[日本三代実録]]』に、「貞観元年己卯（875年）正月二十七 日大和国従五位下、長尾神社従五位上」とあり、9世紀にはこの地に鎮座していた。社伝によると、寛平9年（897年）より弘安4年（1281年）までの間に九階進昇。弘安4年には正二位、江戸時代には正一位にまで昇進した。『放光寺古今縁起』（1302年）や『大和志』（1736年）によると祭神は[[伊勢神宮|伊勢の神宮]]の[[皇大神宮|内宮]]及び[[外宮]]の大神[[天照大神]]、[[豊受大神]]である。伊勢の神宮の真西に鎮座していることから、古くから人々の篤い信仰を得た神社であった。また住吉・熱田・諏訪の神々も祀られている。江戸期の文献では[[水光姫命]]、[[白雲別命]]も祀られたとある。また『放光寺古今縁起』には「長尾神社は葛下郡全体の総社である[[天武天皇]]が[[壬申の乱]]（672年）で勝利した後に感謝の気持ちから葛下郡一郡を当社に献じられた」と記されている。 鎮座地は[[竹内街道]]、[[長尾街道]]、[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]が交差する交通の要衝であり、古来より交通安全、旅行安全の神として篤く信仰されている。[[参道]]は[[拝殿]]に向かって東西に長く伸びており一の鳥居は近年新たに竣工した。 二の鳥居の両脇には「なで蛙」が配されて、参拝者を見守っており、安産祈願の神としても名高い。 また水光姫命は[[古事記]]や[[日本書紀]]に体が光って尾が生じていたと記されており、神様の化身が[[白蛇]]であると言われるところから蛇の頭が[[大神神社]]で尾が'''長尾神社'''という伝承がある。さらに'''長尾神社'''は西日本の[[長尾]][[姓]]の発祥地とも表記がされているが、[[苗字帯刀]]の頃より吉川家が代々受け継いでおり、関係性は定かではない。 ==祭神== 主祭神は[[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ）、[[豊受大神]]（とようけのおおみかみ）。祭神は水光姫命（みひかひめのみこと）、白雲別命（しらくもわけのみこと）。 水光姫命は『[[日本書紀]]』で[[神武天皇]][[神武東征|東征]]に際し吉野川上（奈良県[[川上村 (奈良県)|川上村]]井光）に巡幸の際、[[井戸]]の中から現れた[[天津神・国津神|国神]]（くにつかみ）として記される[[井氷鹿]]で、[[水神]]・井戸の神である。[[古事記]]や[[日本書紀]]によると、光って尾が生じていたと記されている。[[新撰姓氏録]]では「吉野氏の祖先で、天白雲別命の娘・豊御富登であり、水光姫の名は[[神武天皇]]が授けられたもの」としている。社伝では、水光姫命は[[応神天皇]]の治世に三角岩（葛城市竹内）に降臨し、子孫の[[加彌比加尼]]（かむひかね）に命じて長尾に祀らせたもので、姿は白蛇であって、今、神社の東北に藤をもって覆われている御陰井の藤の花がそれであるという。 嘉吉3年（1443年）の王寺町放光寺縁起に「長尾神社は放光寺の鎮守神」と記載されており、長尾神社の名称の由来は、「城上郡（今の桜井市のあたり）の美女を嫁にされた神があったが、一向に姿を現さないので、嫁の父が神の着物のすそに長い赤糸を取り付けてその後をつけていった所、長尾宮（ながおのみや）に入った」という話がある。水光姫命は、豊かな吉野川の水を守る水神であり、井戸の神でもある。 白雲別命は水光姫命の父神で、天より降臨したといわれる。神名から水神・[[雲]]の神とされる。 摂社は[[厳嶋神社]]（市杵嶋姫命（いちきしまのひめのみこと）・配祀　天児屋根命）。[[弁才天|弁財天]]を祀ることから知恵の神とされる。 ==伝説== 昔、大和に大きな[[ヘビ|蛇]]が住み、[[三輪山]]を七回り半に取り巻き、その尾は[[長尾]]一帯まで届いていた。ナガモノ（蛇）を祀りその最後尾であることからナガモノの尾、すなわち[[長尾]]（ナガオ）と名が与えられたという説がある。このことから[[大神神社|三輪明神]]（[[桜井市]]）が頭で、長尾神社はその尾にあたると言われており、一緒詣りをすると利益（りやく）があるとされる。 また、[[竜]]が住んでいたとも言われ、[[石園座多久虫玉神社|竜王社]]（[[大和高田市]]）は竜の頭であり、長尾神社は竜の尾にあたるとされている。長尾神社の手水舎を司るのが竜である。また、竜王社が大蛇の胴体であり、[[大神神社]]−[[石園座多久虫玉神社|竜王社]]−長尾神社が大蛇の化身であるという説もある。そのため、三輪明神と龍王宮の拝殿や本殿は西を、長尾神社の本殿は東で向き合っていると言われる。 以上のような伝説から近年では龍神、白大蛇の神々の宿る神聖な巡礼地（[[スピリチュアル]]スポット）としても信仰が厚い。 ==主な祭事== 1月1日[[歳旦祭]]（午前0時ー本殿初祈祷）、2月3日[[節分祭]]、3月4日[[御田祭|おんだ祭]]（[[御田植祭|御田植神事]]と[[御供]]蒔）、7月中旬[[夏祭り|夏祭]]、10月4日[[宵宮]]（初[[絵馬]]奉納）、10月第２土曜日曜[[だんじり]]、11月23日[[新嘗祭]]（初穂祈祷） == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[奈良県]][[葛城市]]長尾471 ; 交通アクセス {{underline|鉄道}} * 最寄駅：[[近畿日本鉄道]][[南大阪線]] [[磐城駅]]下車 改札左手方向にまっすぐ（拝殿まで徒歩約1分） - 準急、普通のみ停車。列車本数は年末年始を除いて1時間2本程度であり、注意が必要。 * 最寄駅：近畿日本鉄道南大阪線 [[尺土駅]]下車 （拝殿まで徒歩約10分） -特急、急行、 準急、普通すべて停車。一の鳥居をくぐり、参道を歩みながら二の鳥居、拝殿へと進むことができる。 ==関連項目== *[[伊勢神宮]]− 神宮から見て真西に長尾神社が鎮座している。 *[[橿原神宮]] *[[神武天皇]] *[[天武天皇]] *[[ヤマタノオロチ]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} *[http://www.meguru-nara.jp/theme/inori/syaji/05/06.html 長尾神社] - 奈良県観光局 *[http://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/index.cfm/14,10470,53,214,html 長尾神社] - 葛城市 *[http://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/index.cfm/14,2591,43,199,html 長尾神社のおんだ祭] - 葛城市 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかおしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:葛下郡]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大なかおしんしや]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagao]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[Jinja (shrine)|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagao Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagao]], [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[Jinja (shrine)|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]]

Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[Jinja (shrine)|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:葛下郡]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]]

It is located in the city of [[Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:葛下郡]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:葛下郡]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|31.7|N|135|42|37.9|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagao|Nagao]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q6958481]] Wikidata name: Nagao Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = File:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.50880556|N|135.71052778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagao|Nagao]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi|qid=Q659672}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q6958481]] Wikidata name: Nagao Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = File:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.50880556|N|135.71052778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagao|Nagao]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi|qid=Q659672}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Nagao Shrine]] [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Nagao Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q6958481]] Wikidata name: Nagao Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = File:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.50880556|N|135.71052778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagao|Nagao]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi|qid=Q659672}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}}

Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}}
