[[sn:commons:Category:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[ja:長尾神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6958481]] Wikidata name: Nagao Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = File:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.50880556|N|135.71052778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagao|Nagao]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi|WD=Q659672}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

長尾神社 | image = File:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.50880556|N|135.71052778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagao|Nagao]], [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]]
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi|WD=Q659672}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It is located in the city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]] in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ([[:en:Omamori|Omamori]]) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[ja:長尾神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[ja:長尾神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)]][[en:Nagao Shrine]][[ja:長尾神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Nagao Shrine
長尾神社 | image = File:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara) haiden.jpg | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' or hall of worship | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.50880556|N|135.71052778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shikinai Grand Shrine | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Nagao|Nagao]], {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|WD=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi}}
Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi|WD=Q659672}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|WD=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ({{ill|Omamori|en|Omamori|WD=Q1756788|lt=Omamori}}) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|WD=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ({{ill|Omamori|en|Omamori|WD=Q1756788|lt=Omamori|1=Omamori}}) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Nagao Shrine]] [[ja:長尾神社]] {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q6958481}} [[Category:Buildings in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

Nara, Japan 639-2164 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi|qid=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} }} {{nihongo|'''Nagao Shrine'''|長尾神社|Nagao-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to the goddesses [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu-ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyo-uke| Toyouke-ōmikami]], [[:en:Mihika-hime|Mihikahime-mikoto]] and [[:en:Shirakumo-wake|Shirakumowake-mikoto]]. It is located in the city of {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|qid=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. ==Sandō== In typical [[Shinto]] style the outermost [[Torii|gate]], Ichi-no-torii, leads to the second gate, Ni-no-torii, through a 200 meter long path called a sandō. ==Temizusha== After crossing the sandō, a traditional Shinto style road, pilgrims to the shrine encounter the Temizusha, a small, roofed structure containing a pool of water for use in ritual purification. The god of this pool is Dragon. Prayers purify their hands and purify their mouths there as a symbolic act to purify the mind and body. The second of two torii gates stands just behind the Temizusha. ==Haiden== This spiritual hall for visitors, located just after the second large torii gate, is open to the public for the offering of prayers to the god ([[Kami]]), the giving of donations, and the purchase of special talismans of protection, such as amulets ({{ill|Omamori|en|Omamori|qid=Q1756788|lt=Omamori|1=Omamori}}) and hanging scrolls ([[ofuda]]). On special days, one may be invited to a [[kagura]]. The [[miko]] (shrine maiden) guides into the [[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]. Lay down your head totally and [[:en:Kan-Nushi|Kan-Nushi]] (Shinto priest) purifies us. After that, Oriental Miko dance in sync with the music playing bell(Suzu), flute (Ryu-teki), taiko, and Gagaku. Urayasu-no-Mai (means 'dance at the seashore') is famous in Nagao shrine. You pass from item by Hatsuho Ryou prayer from Kan-Nushi, the prayer is finished. Especial of one, we can get to commemorate the dish made of earthenware after the reception as a special shrine, Miko serve a sacred Omiki (sake of the gods). ==Matsuri festivals== *1 January - Saitan-Sai (New Year Shinto Festival, Miko) *4 March - Onda-Matsuri (Taue praying for the rich harvest, Gokumaki(mochi-maki)) *around 15 July - Natsu-Matsuri (pray for dynamic summer, Karaoke) *4 October - YoMiya (for newborn children, Mystic Miko Dance) *2 October Saturday and Sunday - Danjiri{{Clarify|date=September 2013|reason=Should this be out of order?}} *23 November - Niiname-Sai (thanksgiving day for rich harvest, New Kome) ==External links== {{Commons Category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [https://wayback.archive-it.org/all/20141230150900/http://www.isejingu.or.jp/english/ Ise Jingu] * [http://wakajishi.web.fc2.com/] {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|長尾神社|version=102720413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{Commons category|Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q6958481|co|commons:Category:Nagao-jinja (Katsuragi, Nara)|en|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Katsuragi, Nara]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Katsurashimo District]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
